#: This gets merged into every version as #/local.
The zeroth element of this is always null since there is no version 0.

source.description: The address of the entity sending packets in this test

source-node.description: The address of the source pScheduler node, if different

dest.description: The address of the entity receiving packets in this test

dest-node.description: The address of the destination pScheduler node, if different

duration.description: The length of time to run the test

packet-count.description: The number of packets to send before reporting a result

packet-interval.description: The number of seconds to delay between sending packets

packet-timeout.description: The number of seconds to wait before declaring a packet lost

packet-padding.description: The size of padding to add to the packet in bytes

ctrl-port.description: The control plane port to use for the entity acting as the server (the dest if flip is not set, the source otherwise)

data-ports.description: The port range to use on the side of the test running the client. At least two ports required.

ip-tos.description: DSCP value for TOS byte in the IP header as an integer

ip-version.description: Force a specific IP address type used performing the test. Useful when specifying hostnames as source or dest that may map to both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses.

bucket-width.description: The bin size to use for histogram calculations. This value is divided into the result as reported in seconds and truncated to the nearest 2 decimal places.

output-raw.description: Output individual packet statistics. This will substantially increase the size of a successful result.

flip.description: In multi-participant mode, have the dest start the client and request a reverse test. Useful in some firewall and NAT environments.

source-node.description: The address of the source pScheduler node, if different

dest.description: The address of the entity receiving packets in this test

dest-node.description: The address of the destination pScheduler node, if different

protocol.description: The protocol to use in making the measurement

packet-timeout.description: The number of seconds to wait before declaring a packet lost

bucket-width.description: The bin size to use for histogram calculations. This value is divided into the result as reported in seconds and truncated to the nearest 2 decimal places.

output-raw.description: Output individual packet statistics. This will substantially increase the size of a successful result.
